summary: axois-utils performs install-time host reconnaissance and HTTP exfiltration
details: The package's postinstall.js (a lifecycle script that runs automatically on `npm install`) imports `child_process` and uses `http.request` to POST host- and user-identifying data collected from the installer's machine. The collected fields include `os.hostname()`, the username, and the current working directory (postinstall.js lines 13, 63, 66-67), which are then placed into a request body and sent via an outbound POST (lines 144-148). The package name is also a likely typosquat of the popular `axios` HTTP client, increasing the chance of accidental installation. There is no legitimate reason for a utility package to harvest hostname/username/cwd and beacon them to a remote endpoint during install.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
